---
title: "Positron Extensions"
toc: true
toc-location: right
---

> **Why?** Positron extensions add functionality to the Positron code editor,
  enabling features like running Stata code directly from Positron and giving
  AI agents context of your coding environment. Installing following extensions
  is essential for a seamless AI-assisted coding experience with Stata.

**What you need to do:**

- Open Positron and navigate to the Extensions panel to install [Positron Stata](https://open-vsx.org/vscode/item?itemName=ntluong95.positron-stata) extension and other optional extensions.
- Set the [Stata installation Path](vscode-server://settings/positron.stata.installationPath) in Positron settings to enable Stata integration.
- Verify Stata runs correctly from Positron

## Extensions overview

Positron/VS Code is a general-purpose editor — it ships with minimal built-in
functionality by design. Extensions allow it to support any language, tool, or
workflow without bloating the core application.

Some notable extensions include:

- [Positorn assistant](https://open-vsx.org/vscode/item?itemName=posit.assistant) - installed by default, it provides AI code suggestions and completions using github copilot and other AI tools.
- [Claude Code](https://open-vsx.org/vscode/item?itemName=Anthropic.claude-code) for agentic AI coding usinhg Anthropi's Claude LLMs.
- [Positorn Databot](https://open-vsx.org/vscode/item?itemName=posit.databot) for AI-assisted data analysis and visualization

And many other. see: [Open VSX Registry](https://open-vsx.org/) for a comprehensive list of available extensions.

### Installing any extensions

Open the **Extensions panel** with `Ctrl+Shift+X` or click the square icon ![Extensions icon](../../assets/img/extensions.png) on the left sidebar.

To install any extension: **search by name** → click **Install** → **reload Positron** if prompted (`Ctrl+Shift+P` → `Developer: Reload Window`).

### Poistoron - Stata integration

Stata has no native editor integration, so extensions like Stata for Positron
bridge that gap by handling the communication between Positron and Stata's backend.
This is essential for a seamless AI-assisted coding experience with Stata.

Positron-Stata integration relies on three components working together:

1. Stata 18+ with Model Context Protocol (MCP) support.
2. Python and `uv` that sends code to Stata and retrieves logs as output.
3. Positron with extension that integrates results in the user interface.

Running Stata code from Positron brings following **limitations** to be aware of:

- custom logs in Stata `log using <filename>, clear` is **not supported**
  because extensions reli on Stata's built-in logging to capture output and
  errors and Stata does not allow multiple log files written at once.

- only code in `.do` files can be executed, support if `.ado` development
  is limited.

- Bugs are possible. If you encounter any issues, please report them to the
  extension developers (see links below).


### Recommended extensions

There are 2 recent extensions to enable Stata integration with Positron. They are
equivalent and offer similar features. Differences are in the degree of their
maturity and tools that the offer.

- **I recommend installing [Stata for Positron](https://open-vsx.org/extension/ntluong95/positron-stata)** as it offer a more seamless integration.
- The [Stata MCP](https://open-vsx.org/extension/DeepEcon/stata-mcp) is more mature but offers less convenient integration. It is a good alternative if you encounter issues with the Stata for Positron extension.

Both are explained below. [You only need to install one of them]{style="background-color: #27ae60; color: white; padding: 2px 4px;"}.

Finally, there are extension for Stata syntax highlighting:

- [stataglow](https://open-vsx.org/vscode/item?itemName=randrescastaneda.stataglow)


## Stata for Positron

Help and documentation is here: [github.com/ntluong95/positron-stata](https://github.com/ntluong95/positron-stata)

1. Open the Extensions panel (`Ctrl+Shift+X`)

2. Search for **Stata for Positron** or `ntluong95.positron-stata`

3. Click **Install**

4. Reload Positron: `Ctrl+Shift+P` → `Developer: Reload Window`

5. Open settings in positron (`Ctrl+,`).

6. Search for [`positron.stata.installationPath`](vscode-server://settings/positron.stata.installationPath)

7. Set the path to your Stata installation, e.g. `C:\Program Files\StataNow19\` (adjust if Stata is installed elsewhere).

8. Create a new `.do` file and run examplary code to verify the extension is working.


### Stata for Positron features

::: .panel-tabset



:::






## Stata MCP Extension

The [Stata MCP extension](https://open-vsx.org/extension/DeepEcon/stata-mcp) enables **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** for Stata. It lets you:

- Run Stata `.do` files directly from Positron
- Give AI agents (GitHub Copilot, Claude) full context of your Stata environment
- See Stata output inside Positron without switching windows

### Install

1. Open the Extensions panel (`Ctrl+Shift+X`)
2. Search for **Stata MCP**
3. Click **Install**
4. Reload Positron: `Ctrl+Shift+P` → `Developer: Reload Window`

### Configure

Open settings (`Ctrl+,`) and update the two Stata settings:

| Setting | Value | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| `stata-vscode.stataPath` | `C:\Program Files\StataNow19\` | Adjust if Stata is installed elsewhere |
| `stata-vscode.stataEdition` | `mp` | Use `se` or `be` if you have a different edition |

### Verify

Create a new `.do` file and enter:

```stata
sysuse auto
summarize price
display "Stata is working!"
```

| # | Action |
|---|--------|
| 1 | Save the file (`Ctrl+S`) — **unsaved edits are not executed** |
| 2 | Run with `Ctrl+Shift+D`, or `Ctrl+Shift+P` → `Stata: Run Current File` |
| 3 | Check output: `Ctrl+Shift+P` → `Output: Show Output Channels` → `Stata` |

::: callout-tip
You can drag the Output panel to a different position in Positron to keep it visible alongside your code.
:::

---

## Other Recommended Extensions

*Placeholder — add further extensions as the course expands (e.g., Quarto, Python, R-related).*
